meta.disclaimer: Synthetic data — for demonstration only. Not real Veterans, providers, or claims.
meta.notes: NPIs deliberately fail the NPI check digit; TINs use the 00- prefix. Amounts are illustrative CMS-allowed proxies.

rules.name: NCCI Procedure-to-Procedure edit
rules.description: 43235 is a component of 43239 and not separately payable in the same session.

rules.name: Medically Unlikely Edit
rules.description: Units billed exceed the medically unlikely threshold for the code.

rules.name: Modifier 59 override review
rules.description: Modifier 59 applied to bypass a PTP edit without documented distinct service.

rules.name: Multiple Procedure Payment Reduction
rules.description: Reduces payment for the second and subsequent procedures in the same session.

rules.name: Fee schedule / pricing validation
rules.description: Validates paid amount against the applicable VA fee schedule / CMAC allowance.

rules.name: Duplicate claim detection
rules.description: Claim duplicates a previously adjudicated claim (same provider, patient, date, code).

rules.name: Referral/authorization mismatch
rules.description: Billed service does not match the authorized referral.

rules.name: Pay-and-report threshold
rules.description: Claim paid with appended flag metadata and routed to the analyst workload module for post-payment review.

models.name: E/M Peer-Group Profile
models.description: Flags providers whose E/M level distribution deviates from specialty peers.

models.name: Per-Patient Frequency
models.description: Flags procedure frequency far above per-patient norms.

models.name: Modifier Abuse Pattern
models.description: Flags abnormal modifier-59 override rates vs peers.

models.name: Residential LOS & Network
models.description: Flags clusters of just-under-threshold residential stays with shared patients/registration across facilities.

xai.summary: E/M level distribution deviates 5.8σ from the Internal Medicine peer group: 99215 share 90% vs peer median 14%, sustained across 11 months. Linked diagnoses show low clinical complexity. No rules fired — flagged by a composite ML/AI anomaly model.

NCCI Procedure-to-Procedure edit: 43235 is bundled into 43239 and not separately payable in the same session. Modifier 59 applied on 28/31 paired claims to bypass the edit; documentation does not support a distinct procedural service. Override rate 90% vs peer 6%.

allegations.narrative: Requested medical record. Patient has ESRD (N18.6) with standing order for thrice-weekly in-center hemodialysis.

xai.summary: Per-patient procedure frequency 6.2σ above norm: CPT 90935 billed 36× in 90 days for a single patient. Confidence is low — frequency alone, no corroborating anomaly. Review of the medical record indicates a standing dialysis regimen consistent with ESRD.
Modifier misuse flagged for review. Estimated post-payment exposure $9,240.
Duplicate claim flagged for review. Estimated post-payment exposure $6,010.

Modifier misuse flagged for review. Estimated post-payment exposure $1,210.

Sonoran Recovery Center is one of 4 residential facilities under a single holding company (Meridian Behavioral Holdings LLC, officer Marcus D. Feld) that cycle the same veterans across AZ/CA/NV for back-to-back stays kept just under the 30-day threshold — banking near-full per-diem charges on each readmission. Shared patients with 4 cross-facility overlaps; separate TINs mask the common ownership.

New 27-day residential stay from a facility in the flagged Meridian Behavioral chain — the same veteran was discharged from the AZ facility 26 days ago. Deny before the per-diem is paid.
43235 unbundled from 43239 with modifier 59 — the same NCCI-PTP pattern this TIN was flagged for post-pay. Deny the component line.
Level-5 E/M on an established hypertension visit from a provider billing 99215 on ~90% of visits. Hold for records before paying.
Oxygen concentrator billed twice within 30 days for the same veteran. Duplicate — deny the second.
8 therapeutic-exercise units billed for a home-health visit with no matching visit note on file. Hold pending documentation.
Third brain MRI for this veteran in 60 days. Hold for prior-imaging review.
EKG + E/M billed same day; modifier logic checks out and the amount matches the fee schedule. Low risk — clear to pay.
Major-joint injection consistent with the referral and fee schedule. No anomaly — auto-pay candidate.
Routine established-patient visit. Clean — clear to pay.

precedents.note: E/M level inflation confirmed; provider education + partial recovery.
Documentation supported higher-complexity visits; no overpayment.
NCCI PTP violation with modifier 59; full recovery.
Component billing of a comprehensive code; partial recovery after appeal.
ESRD dialysis regimen; thrice-weekly frequency clinically appropriate.
Excessive repeat imaging without documented indication.
Modifier 25 misuse on E/M billed with a procedure.
Services billed after date of death; recovered and referred.
Services not rendered; referred to OIG.
